content.full_text:
Gil Garcetti
Los Angeles County
District Attorney
18000 Criminal Courts Building
210 West Temple Street
Los Angeles, CA  90012-3210

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-93-042

Dear Mr. Garcetti:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding your responsibilities pursuant to the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ governing the legally permissible use of campaign funds.

QUESTION

     As Los Angeles County District Attorney, may you use your campaign funds to pay the membership dues for the local bar associations within your jurisdiction?

CONCLUSION

     As Los Angeles County District Attorney, you may use campaign funds to pay the membership dues for the local bar associations within your jurisdiction.

FACTS

     You were elected as Los Angeles County District Attorney on November 3, 1992. You currently hold unused campaign funds in the

_______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA  95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-93-042
Page 2


campaign bank account established for that election..

You stated that traditionally, the Los Angeles County
District Attorney has been asked to hold membership and
participate in local bar associations within the jurisdiction.
Local bar associations generally charge membership fees. For
example, the Los Angeles County Bar Association requires $110 in
annual dues.

You have asked whether you may use campaign funds to pay the
membership dues for the various local bar associations within your
jurisdiction.

ANALYSIS

The "personal use" law now requires that any expenditure of
campaign funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.)
Moreover, expenditures which confer a substantial personal
benefit² must be directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.³ (Section 89512.)

While the payment of membership dues for civic and
professional organizations is not expressly set forth in the
personal use statutes, we have advised that such expenditures are
permitted. (Eckel Advice Letter, No. A-91-488.)

According to your facts, the Los Angeles County District
Attorney has traditionally been asked to hold membership and
participate in local bar associations within the jurisdiction.
Local bar associations generally charge membership fees. You
stated you wished to join some of the local bar associations
within your jurisdiction, including the Los Angeles County Bar
Association which requires $110 in annual dues.

In light of your position, membership in local bar
associations within your jurisdiction would be directly related to
a political or governmental purpose associated with your office.

_________________
2  Section 89511(b)(3) provides that a "substantial personal
benefit" means "an expenditure of campaign funds which results in
a direct personal benefit with a value of more than one hundred
dollars ($100) to a candidate, elected officer, or any individual
or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of
campaign funds held by a committee."

3  Other expenditures are expressly governed by the "directly
related" standard as set forth in the personal use statutes. (See
e.g., Section 89513.) Additionally, where campaign funds become
surplus campaign funds, they are subject to more restrict rules.
(Section 89519.) However, your campaign funds are not considered
"surplus campaign funds" until you leave office or after you are
defeated in an election to office.

Our File No. A-93-042
Page 3


Thus, you may use campaign funds to pay the membership dues for
the local bar associations within your jurisdiction.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.4

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division


4 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
